name: Android App Reverse Engineering to API Abuse
description: Attack tree modelling reverse engineering of an Android application to discover and abuse trusted backend APIs
root_objective: Reverse engineer Android app and abuse trusted backend APIs to access unauthorized data

nodes.title: Abuse Trusted Backend APIs via Reversed Android App
nodes.description: Gain unauthorized access to backend data and functionality by reverse engineering the Android application

nodes.title: Reverse Engineer APK
nodes.description: Decompile and analyze the Android APK to understand application logic, API endpoints, and security mechanisms

nodes.title: Decompile APK with jadx/apktool
nodes.description: Use jadx or apktool to decompile the APK and recover Java/Kotlin source code and resources

nodes.title: Extract Hardcoded Secrets
nodes.description: Search decompiled code for API keys, tokens, certificates, and hardcoded credentials

nodes.title: Map API Endpoints and Auth Flow
nodes.description: Identify all backend API endpoints, authentication mechanisms, and request signing logic

nodes.title: Bypass Client-Side Protections
nodes.description: Defeat certificate pinning, root detection, and integrity checks

nodes.title: Bypass Certificate Pinning
nodes.description: Use Frida or Objection to bypass certificate pinning and intercept HTTPS traffic

nodes.title: Bypass Root/Integrity Detection
nodes.description: Patch or hook root detection and integrity checking routines to run on a modified device

nodes.title: Abuse Backend API Trust
nodes.description: Exploit the backend's implicit trust in client requests to access unauthorized data or functionality

nodes.title: IDOR via Manipulated API Requests
nodes.description: Modify user/object identifiers in API requests to access other users' data

nodes.title: Abuse Hidden Admin Endpoints
nodes.description: Call administrative API endpoints discovered during reverse engineering that lack proper authorization

nodes.title: Exploit Insecure Local Storage
nodes.description: Extract sensitive data stored insecurely on the device

nodes.title: Read Shared Preferences / SQLite DBs
nodes.description: Access unencrypted shared preferences, SQLite databases, and local files containing tokens or PII
